results.title: AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
highlights:
AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
[...]
AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
[...]
Occidental Petroleum
[...]
# AI Machine Learning Engineer
3 days agoHouston, TexasMid-LevelSeniorML / AI
[...]
LLMAIDeep LearningRelational DatabasesCloudModel TrainingData EngineeringCausal Inference
[...]
Research and build AI/ML systems for oil and gas applications including subsurface characterization and reservoir engineering at Occidental Petroleum's AI Center of Excellence in Houston.
[...]
## Full Job Description
[...]
We are looking for an experienced and innovative individual contributor to fill the position of **AI Machine Learning Engineer **within our **AI Center of Excellence** group based in **Houston, TX. **The incumbent will research, build, and design artificial intelligence systems to automate predictive models, and design machine learning systems, models and schemes. 
**Essential Job Responsibilities**
[...]
* Work with Subject Matter Experts to design appropriate machine learning model types and architectures for particular applications ranging from subsurface characterization, reservoir engineering, production, and facilities etc
* Tune algorithm hyper-parameters and perform model training and validation
* Create workflows to optimize performance for computationally heavy workloads
* Collaborate with Machine Learning Operation team to deploy machine learning solutions to the cloud
[...]
**Qualifications**
[...]
* Bachelor's degree in computer science, mathematics, physics, engineering or related field required
* Master's degree is highly preferred. Equivalent combination of advanced education and relevant experience will be considered.
* A minimum of 5 years of experience and deep knowledge in orchestration methods and tools to optimally use computational infrastructure, and machine learning methods and algorithms
* Strong Python or R programming skills required
* Experience developing, architecting, and running ML or deep learning workload in the Cloud.
* Ability to express the intuition behind basic ML algorithms
* Experience performing basic hyper parameter optimization
* Ability to follow model training, deployment, and operational best practices
* Experience with Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for orchestration
* Knowledge of Terraform and Ansible for automating infrastructure management
* Knowledge of YAML for automating model and application code deployment
* Understanding of data engineering principles and databases (relational, NoSQL)
* Strong critical thinking and communication skills
**Additional Desired Qualifications:**
[...]
* AWS certification is highly preferred, with a strong preference for the AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty credential.
* Experience with Bayesian statistical modeling, including Bayesian inference, Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), and causal inference.
* Hands‑on experience in geospatial data analytics using Esri products, with a focus on ArcGIS Enterprise.
* Experience with LLM-powered applications such as chatbots, question-answering systems, document processing and agentic workflow design.
[...]
Apply on Occidental PetroleumView Original Posting
[...]
### Machine Learning Engineer, AWS Identity Analytics Platform
[...]
Machine Learning Engineer

results.title: AI and Machine-Learning Engineer (multiple positions) - Aker BP
highlights:
AI and Machine-Learning Engineer (multiple positions) - Aker BP
[...]
# AI and Machine-Learning Engineer (multiple positions)
[...]
Join our AI team to design, build, and productionise cutting-edge ML, AI, and agentic AI solutions, driving innovation across various specialisations from agent engineering to AI systems integration and product development.
[...]
Experience building and shipping ML or AI-powered systems, ideally including LLM-based applications or ML solutions in production systems.
[...]
We are hiring engineers across several tracks to build, integrate, and productionise AI and agentic AI solutions within our cross-functional delivery pods. All roles share a common core – building production-grade AI systems – but differ in specialisation.
[...]
Build and ship production AI and agentic AI features within a cross-functional pod.
[...]
Design, evaluate, and harden agent workflows, APIs, and AI-powered integrations depending on your track.
[...]
We encourage you to apply with a preferred specialisation and an openness to discuss adjacent tracks. We assess core engineering strength first, and match specialisation based on pod needs and candidate strengths. We assess against a shared senior profile and match track based on strengths. Indicate a preferred track or stay open to placement. Available specialisations include: Track A – Agent Engineer / LLM Engineer, Track B – AI Systems / Integration Engineer, and Track C – AI Product Engineer.
[...]
Track A: Agent Engineer / LLM Engineer
[...]
Design, build, and productionise AI agents that reason, orchestrate tools, and integrate with enterprise platforms. Focus on agentic patterns (ReAct, plan-execute, critic-actor), multi-agent coordination, guardrails, and evaluation. Significant emphasis on automating the data product lifecycle through agents.
[...]
Experience with multi-agent coordination patterns (planner-executor, critic-actor, etc.). – Proven ability to develop and deploy LLM applications with a strong focus on robustness, uncertainty, and performance. – Contributions to open-source LLM projects or relevant research publications.
[...]
Track B: AI Systems / Integration Engineer
[...]
Design and build production-grade AI systems including agent orchestration, APIs, and integration with enterprise platforms. Focus on reliability, scalability, and secure execution of AI workflows across our ecosystem (CDF, Fabric, ADME/OSDU, Integration Platform).
[...]
Strong API design background, including RESTful and GraphQL. – Extensive experience with event-driven architectures (e.g., Kafka, Azure Service Bus). – Proficiency in enterprise integration patterns and data orchestration within large-scale systems.
[...]
Track C: AI Product Engineer
[...]
Operate at the intersection of domain, AI, and engineering. Design, prototype, and implement AI solutions; iterate rapidly with users; ensure solutions are practical, scalable, and aligned with evaluation and platform standards. Strong user empathy and prototyping chops matter most here.
[...]
Experience bringing AI-powered products from concept to market. – Proficiency in A/B testing and quantitative analysis for product iteration. – Deep understanding of specific industrial domains, e.g., energy, manufacturing, or logistics.
[...]
Our ambition is
[...]
: to become the world’s
[...]
‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model. And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

results.title: Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions) - Aker BP
highlights:
Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions) - Aker BP
[...]
# Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions)
[...]
Join our established AI team, bringing scientific depth and rigor to drive innovation across diverse specializations, from agentic AI to industrial vision and signal machine learning, and ensure robust, uncertainty-aware production systems.
[...]
We are seeking Senior AI and ML Scientists across several critical specialisations to bring scientific depth to our established AI organization. These roles are pivotal in raising evaluation rigor, mentoring across the team, and taking ownership of the specific modalities our industrial use cases demand.
[...]
You will apply scientific rigor across all stages of AI delivery, from initial problem framing through to evaluation design. This involves technical deep dives within your chosen specialization, contributing to our collective knowledge, and raising the bar across the team. A key aspect is mentoring data scientists and engineers, and actively moving research into production through collaboration with MLOps, architecture, and pod engineers. You will also contribute to establishing uncertainty quantification standards, understanding how prediction confidence and calibration affect safe automation thresholds, and promoting software engineering discipline in scientific work, including version-controlled experiments, peer-reviewed code, and production-ready implementations.
[...]
Track 1: Principal AI / ML Scientist
[...]
Provide scientific leadership across the full team. Set the evaluation bar, mentor across specialisations, shape research direction, and represent the AI function in architectural and strategic decisions. A high-leverage senior hire.
[...]
PhD in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Statistics, or related field — or equivalent demonstrated depth.
[...]
Track record of moving research into production, not just publishing.
[...]
Deep working knowledge of uncertainty quantification, calibration, and out-of-distribution behaviour in autonomous AI.
[...]
Track 2: Senior ML Engineer / Applied Scientist — Language and Agents
[...]
Bring deep expertise in LLM-based systems, agentic reasoning, RAG quality, evaluation frameworks for probabilistic outputs, and multi-agent coordination. Translate uncertainty in LLM outputs into concrete risk thresholds and operational guardrails.
[...]
Hands-on production experience shipping ML or LLM-based applications, ideally including agentic patterns.
[...]
Demonstrated work on evaluation methodology for probabilistic and non-deterministic outputs.
[...]
Hands-on ML and AI architecture experience including evaluation of quality performance.
[...]
checks and re-training.
[...]
Experience with calibrated uncertainty estimation in production.
[...]
Track 3: Senior Signal Processing / Acoustic ML Engineer
[...]
Specialist depth in acoustic signal processing and related machine-learning approaches. Relevant across subsurface, operations, and equipment monitoring use cases.
[...]
Track 4: Senior Computer Vision Engineer / Scientist
[...]
MSc or PhD in Computer Vision, Machine Learning, or related
[...]
Our ambition is clear: to become the world’s first AI‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
AI-native E&P company.
[...]
We didn't discover AI last year. For a decade, we've been building the data foundation and interoperable ecosystem that now lets us scale AI across some of the most complex operations in our industry.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model. And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

results.title: AI Solutions Engineer - Aker BP
highlights:
AI Solutions Engineer - Aker BP
[...]
# AI Solutions Engineer
[...]
Are you an AI Solutions Engineer who thrives on translating complex business problems into tangible, production-ready AI solutions within a dynamic, cross-functional environment? Join us and anchor one of our AI delivery pods, making a real impact on our journey to become the first AI-native E&P company.
[...]
Strong technical hands-on background in AI/ML engineering, data engineering, or AI solutions delivery.
[...]
Proven ability to lead and deliver production-ready AI systems, with a strong track record of shipping LLM-based or agentic solutions.
[...]
Deep understanding of probabilistic AI outputs, operational risk, and designing safeguards for agentic workflows.
[...]
About the role
[...]
Drive AI Innovation in the Energy Sector.
[...]
We are building seven cross-functional AI delivery pods aligned to business domains. Each pod needs a technical anchor – someone who can lead end-to-end AI use-case delivery while partnering closely with business domain experts, data engineering, and central AI functions.
[...]
As an AI Solution Engineer, you are the cross-functional glue. You translate business problems into AI solutions, shape technical approach, coordinate with architecture and platform teams, develop and test AI solutions, and make sure what ships actually works in production and adds measurable value.
[...]
Aker BP is building the foundation for agentic AI at scale. We are doing this as one operating model; strategy, architecture, science, data, safety, and delivery designed to work together, not as separate teams. This role is part of that build.
[...]
We are moving AI into the core of industrial operations, work that spans physical assets, regulated environments, and decisions that affect production, safety, and the broader energy system. We work cross-disciplinarily, with a focus on shipping into production rather than running experiments indefinitely.
[...]
Key responsibilities
[...]
AI solution designCollaborate closely with domain specialists to translate business needs into implementable AI solutions.
[...]
Impact-driven prioritisationDrive use-case prioritisation based on data readiness, technical feasibility, and business impact.
[...]
AI delivery and developmentLead the end-to-end technical delivery of AI and agentic AI use cases within your assigned pod.
[...]
Cross-functional coordinationCoordinate with central architecture, MLOps, security, and data governance teams to ensure compliance with enterprise standards.
[...]
Own quality and productionOwn evaluation setup, quality bars, and production readiness, including CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and code-review standards.
[...]
Uncertainty handlingDesign solutions with explicit handling of model uncertainty, defining confidence thresholds, fallback behaviours, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.
[...]
AI-first mindsetWe are an AI-first company, committed to leading our industry in AI adoption and unlocking its transformative power.
[...]
Our ambition is clear: to become the world’s first AI‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
We're hiring to accelerate our journey towards becoming the first AI-native E&P company.
[...]
We didn't discover AI last year. For a decade, we've been building the data foundation and interoperable ecosystem that now lets us scale AI across some of the most complex operations in our industry.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model.
[...]
And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

results.title: Data Scientist - RevOps Careers
highlights:
Data Scientist - RevOps Careers

# Data Scientist

On Site Full Time Malaysia ExxonMobil

ExxonMobil is seeking a Data Scientist to join their Kuala Lumpur team. The successful candidate will work on developing and deploying data science solutions to improve business performance and drive innovation. The role is based in Kuala Lumpur and offers a competitive benefits package.

## Requirements

- Post a Job
- Master’s, or PhD degree in Data Science, Computer Science, IT, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, or related disciplines
- At least 3 years of experience in developing, applying, and validating data-driven tools to model complex systems
- In-depth knowledge and practical experience in statistical analysis techniques and machine learning techniques
- Practical experience in the full machine learning lifecycle, from problem formulation and data acquisition to model building and deployment at enterprise scale
- Deep conceptual and mathematical understanding of algorithms, models, model assessment techniques, solution development and explainable AI

## Benefits

- Competitive benefits package
- Opportunity to work on exciting and challenging projects
- Collaborative and dynamic work environment
- Professional development opportunities

To apply for this job please visit jobs.exxonmobil.com.

results.title: Semantic data modelling and data governance specialists (multiple positions) - Aker BP
highlights:
Semantic data modelling and data governance specialists (multiple positions) - Aker BP
[...]
# Semantic data modelling and data governance specialists (multiple positions)
[...]
Build the semantic and governance foundation that agentic AI depends on — so agents reason from shared enterprise meaning and act on data that is safe to act on.
[...]
Demonstrable hands-on production experience in one of the following specialisations: Semantic Data Modelling, AI-Ready Data Product Ownership, or Governed-by-Design Data Management (see more below)
[...]
A strong understanding of how AI agents consume data, and the crucial role of robust data governance and semantics.
[...]
Deep experience in data governance, data management, or enterprise semantics.
[...]
Build the semantic and governance foundation that agentic AI depends on — so agents reason from shared enterprise meaning and act on data that is safe to act on. These roles sit at the data and semantic foundation layer of the AI operating model — providing the meaning, lineage, and governance contracts that every agent and pod consumes.
[...]
Aker BP is building the foundation for agentic AI at scale. We are doing this as one operating model; strategy, architecture, science, data, safety, and delivery designed to work together, not as separate teams. This role is part of that build.
[...]
Track 1: Enterprise semantic modeller
[...]
Own enterprise meaning as a first-class, machine-usable asset. Ensure that AI agents, analytics, and humans reason using a single coherent understanding of the business. Partner closely with domain experts to capture, maintain, and evolve enterprise semantics. Your expertise and decisions are essential for scaling meaning throughout the business.
[...]
Hands-on production work with semantic web technologies (RDF, OWL, SPARQL) or property-graph equivalents.
[...]
Track 2: AI-ready data product owner
[...]
Own the data catalogue and ensure data products are correctly defined, governed, and maintained in Purview. Provide trusted, well-structured data as the foundation for scalable AI and agent solutions. Bridge between data producers and AI consumers.
[...]
Track 3: Data management lead — governed-by-design
[...]
Make data management a first-class enabler of safe, autonomous AI: gatekeeping use-case prioritisation based on data readiness, ensuring classification and usage terms are machine-readable, building traceability into agent-data interactions, and managing data quality throughout the data lifecycle. Not a compliance-watchdog role — an enabler of trustworthy autonomy with explicit attention to how data completeness and quality directly affect model confidence and downstream risk in agentic workflows.
[...]
Hands-on production work on data classification, lineage, and lifecycle in regulated environments.
[...]
Demonstrated work translating regulatory requirements (EU AI Act, GDPR Art. 10, NIS2) into operational data-management practice.
[...]
Working knowledge of how AI agents consume data and what data-side guardrails they require.
[...]
Our ambition is clear: to become the world’s first AI‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
We're hiring to accelerate our journey towards becoming the first AI-native E&P company.
[...]
We didn't discover AI last year. For a decade, we've been building the data foundation and interoperable ecosystem that now lets us scale AI across some of the most complex operations in our industry.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model. And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

results.title: Data Scientist - (Global) | Jobspawn
highlights:
Data Scientist - (Global) | Jobspawn
[...]
# Data Scientist - (Global)
[...]
Data Scientist (Senior Staff, Staff, & Senior roles available)
[...]
KoBold Metals is a mineral exploration company using AI to explore for the metals we need for our transition to a low-carbon economy. KoBold’s business is discovering, defining, expanding, and developing mineral resources, and KoBold’s objective is to achieve a step-change improvement in exploration success: we aim to discover more tier 1 resources, faster, and with fewer failures.
[...]
Our team includes the best of the industry in exploration geoscience, data science, software engineering, operations, and business personnel. Prior to joining KoBold, KoBold team members have made nearly 20 discoveries. Our exploration programs are co-led by our geoscientists and data scientists, who develop exploration hypotheses, rigorously quantify uncertainty in our understanding of the subsurface, and design data collection programs that most effectively reduce uncertainty, drawing upon a large suite of proprietary exploration technology built by our data scientists and software engineers. Our field programs validate and improve the system and have demonstrated material improvements over conventional exploration methods
[...]
We are hiring a Data Scientists to help accelerate our mission.
[...]
In this role, you will build predictive models and apply a wide range of scientific computing, statistical, and physics-based methods to find places where there is evidence of ore-forming processes at work and to predict the locations of ore-grade mineralization in 2D and 3D. You will help build a worldwide dataset that underlies our exploration program, with careful attention to identifying and quantifying uncertainty in the data and in our predictions. You will create models and develop software to accelerate discovery of critical battery metals.
[...]
You will join an outstanding team of data scientists and engineers and will work closely with KoBold’s world-renowned geoscientists to incorporate our best understanding of the chemical and physical processes that create ore deposits. Working with your geoscience colleagues, you will create 2D and 3D geologic predictions, identify exploration targets, design field programs to collect data, and use that data to reduce the uncertainty in our predictions and guide the next phase of field work.
[...]
Ultimately, your role is to help KoBold make valuable discoveries by building data tools to solve scientific problems. As one of the early members of this team, you will help build these tools from the ground up.
[...]
### Responsibilities
[...]
The Data Scientist will:
[...]
Help develop KoBold’s proprietary software exploration tools.
[...]
Find and curate geophysical, geochemical, geologic, and geographic data and integrate it into KoBold’s proprietary data system.
[...]
Build models to make statistically valid predictions about the locations of compositional anomalies within the Earth’s crust.
[...]
Create effective visualizations for evaluating model performance and enabling rapid interaction with the underlying data and key features.
[...]
Develop and apply data processing, statistical, and physics-based techniques to geoscientific data — from computer vision to geophysical inversions — and use the results to guide our targeting efforts and inform our acquisition and exploration decisions.
[...]
Building predictive models, applying them to different problems, and evaluating and interpreting the results.
[...]
Technical knowledge:
[...]
Broad skills in and knowledge of applied statistics and Bayesian inference
[...]
Substantial understanding of machine learning algorithms
[...]
It is also helpful but not required to have experience with:
[...]
Creating machine learning models on geospatial data
[...]
Image processing or computer vision
[...]
Distributed computing applications for machine learning and other computations

results.title: Digital Technology Experienced Professional – Data Science - MyPetroCareer
highlights:
Digital Technology Experienced Professional – Data Science - MyPetroCareer
[...]
Job Description – Digital Technology Experienced Professional – Data Science
[...]
Baker Hughes stands as a leading global energy-technology company, delivering innovative solutions that make energy safer, cleaner, and more efficient across the entire value chain. Operating in more than 120 countries, the company blends deep industrial expertise with advanced engineering and digital technologies to support energy production, transformation, and decarbonization. With over a century of heritage, Baker Hughes partners with customers in oil and gas, industrial sectors, and emerging energy markets to accelerate progress toward a more sustainable energy future
[...]
As an AI Engineer, you will contribute to the design, development, and deployment of production-grade AI applications, with a strong focus on Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs). You will work closely with experienced AI engineers, data engineers, and specialist product teams to prototype, test, and scale AI-driven capabilities that deliver measurable business value.
[...]
This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, machine learning, and applied AI, and is ideal for an engineer who is excited about building reliable, responsible, and high-quality AI systems rather than standalone models.
[...]
As an AI Engineer, you will be responsible for:
[...]
Assist in developing applications using LLMs and GenAI Tools
[...]
Collaborate with our API Program to ensure quality
[...]
Create, fine tune, and adapt pre-trained models
[...]
Monitor outputs for quality, bias, security, and continued relevance
[...]
Propose and document experiments, prompts, and system designs
[...]
To be successful in this role you will:
[...]
Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience – preferably with a focus on Data and AI
[...]
Be proficient in Python and SQL, with experience working in modern data and analytics environments
[...]
Have a solid understanding of machine learning and natural language processing (NLP) concepts
[...]
Demonstrate hands-on interest or experience with Generative AI and LLM-based
[...]
Have working knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure or GCP)
[...]
Be comfortable collaborating across disciplines, including engineering, product, and architecture teams
[...]
To apply for this job please visit careers.bakerhughes.com.

results.title: Lead Generative AI Specialist | Hirevector
highlights:
Lead Generative AI Specialist | Hirevector
[...]
# Lead Generative AI Specialist
[...]
Posted May 30, 2026
[...]
Are you passionate about improving capabilities, efficiency and performance? Are you interested in responsible for supporting the business owners to lean the process? Join our Digital Technology Team! Our team provides industry-leading products and services that optimize the production and processing of energy. You will contribute to our future as an AI player in the Energy Transformation industry. Partner with the best As a Generative AI Specialist, you will work in teams providing smart solution and addressing problems in a commercial technology and consultancy development environment. In this role, you will contribute to the development and deployment of modern generative AI models, techniques and operational research to leverage the power of data to solve complex problems and push the boundaries of what is possible in the ever-evolving tech landscape. As a Lead Generative AI Specialist, you will be responsible for: Designing and developing of generative AI models, including but not limited to LLMs, VLMs, GANs and Multimodal LLMs. Conducting and driving forward innovative research in the field of generative AI, leading the team in exploring new methodologies and technologies. Working closely with cross-functional teams to integrate new generative models into existing AI systems, ensuring seamless integration and optimizing performance. Developing and testing software components according to customer and technical requirements. Preparing comprehensive reports and presentations on the impact of generative AI initiatives on the business. Fuel your passion T o be successful in this role you will have: Master's degree in computer science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field with a specialization in generative models and proven experience in the development and deployment of generative AI models. PhD is preferred Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work across different disciplines. Proven experience in software programming languages, with a focus on Python, including AI libraries eg PyTorch, TensorFlow, Transformer architecture. Demonstrated skill at function in a team setting Demonstrated awareness of critical thinking and problem solving methods Demonstrated awareness of presentation and influencing skills Ability to analyze and resolve problems through innovation and creative solutions. Natural communication skills, with an ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. Self-motivation that promotes teamwork and collaboration in a cross-functional environment. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working remotely from home or any other work location Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Lead Engineer, Mathematics & Data Science, Research and Technology Development

GenAI Engineer - Associate Data Scientist

Job Description:

Title: AI/ML / Generative AI Engineer

### Location: Irving, Tx (Hybrid - 3 days onsite)

- Strong Data Structures Any Language Code Comprehension Python or Java

results.title: Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher | Hirequorum
highlights:
Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher | Hirequorum

Hirequorum.← All Jobs

# Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher

Posted Jun 6, 2026

### About Terra AI

We are building the state-of-the-art AI platform for the discovery and development of clean energy and mineral resources. We bring the most advanced techniques in generative AI, foundation modeling, and autonomous decision optimization to tackle the most important problems in the geosciences. These systems can help more reliably identify critical resource deposits, more rapidly measure and characterize them, and design more efficient and sustainable production plans.

We are backed by Khosla Ventures and other leading venture investors. We are now looking to grow our team from ~15 to ~30 by the end of the year to continue to mature our technology and support deployment with our world-class mineral and clean energy partners.

Role description

In the same way image generators have shown the remarkable ability to produce a diverse set of realistic pictures conditioned on a text prompt (and other inputs), we are developing a generative model that produces 3D geological models conditioned on geophysical surveys, bore hole measurements, and other forms of physical observation. The outputs of the generative model capture what we know and don’t know about the state of the subsurface, allowing explorers to make maximally informed decisions about how and where to explore for critical resources.

We are looking for a talented deep learning engineer or scientist to lead the development of this model that will revolutionize decision making in the earth subsurface for a wide range of clean energy applications.

Role Responsibilities

- Design, train, test, and iterate on diffusion models for 3D geological models

- Design, train, test, and iterate on an approach to for conditioning generation on geophysical data and other observations

- Inform the generation of synthetic data to improve model performance

- Adapt diffusion modeling approach to specific real-world projects in collaboration with project teams.

### Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

- Extensive PyTorch Experience

- Deep understanding of PyTorch, including writing custom modules, optimizing training, and debugging issues in large-scale models.

- Expertise in Developing Large Deep Learning Models from Scratch

- Proven ability to design, implement, and train complex deep learning architectures from the ground up.

- Data Curation Skills

- Hands-on experience in creating, cleaning, and maintaining high-quality datasets tailored for machine learning applications.

- Strong Software Engineering and Design Experience

- Proficient in software development best practices, including version control, testing, and code optimization.

- Familiarity with designing scalable and maintainable systems.

### Bonus points if you:

- Experience with Generative Models

- Familiarity with generative architectures, particularly diffusion models, and an emphasis on posterior sampling methods.

- Knowledge of Transformer Architectures

- Experience building and training transformers, especially in applications involving 3D data.

- Scaling Models Across Large GPU Clusters

- Expertise in parallelizing models across multiple GPUs and optimizing distributed training pipelines.

- Cloud Infrastructure Expertise

- Experience setting up, managing, and optimizing cloud environments for machine learning workloads, including provisioning resources and managing costs.

Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​

results.title: Senior Data Scientist – Career Development Office | Rice Business
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist – Career Development Office | Rice Business

NOV

# Senior Data Scientist

- Share This:
- Copy Link

JOB DESCRIPTION

The Data and Analytics team works with people across the organization to identify, assess, and solve real business problems by analyzing all available data, including enterprise, connected product, and publicly available datasets, to create and discover meaningful insights. This role builds and deploys solutions that translate operational data into decisions and measurable outcomes.

The Senior Data Scientist acts as a strategic leader in the design, development, and deployment of advanced Data Science and Analytic solutions across NOV’s global operations. This role requires expertise in managing cross-functional projects and communicating insights to executive leadership. The Senior Data Scientist will serve as a thought leader in advancing NOV’s digital and analytics maturity, ensuring data-driven solutions create measurable business impact and support enterprise growth.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will:

- Partner with business leaders to identify high-value opportunities in areas such as demand planning, capacity/production planning, procurement, and financial forecasting
- Develop a firm understanding of specific business processes and real-world workflows
- Work closely with cross-functional teams including domain experts, business analysts, data engineers and software engineers
- Lead data science initiatives that impact operations globally across multiple business units
- Define project scope, timelines and deliverables in collaboration with business leaders
- Architect scalable machine learning and forecasting solutions within cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, Databricks, and Snowflake
- Ensure models are production-ready, robust, and maintainable with appropriate monitoring and retraining pipelines
- Develop standardized methodologies and frameworks to ensure scalability and consistency across diverse environments
- Ensure compliance with data governance, privacy, and security standards while scaling analytics solutions globally
- Manage and mentor interns, junior data scientists and analysts
- Translate advanced analytics results into strategic recommendations for VP and C-level leadership
- Utilize programming languages such as SQL, Python for data manipulation, analysis and model development

SUGGESTED QUALIFICATIONS

- Master’s degree in a quantitative discipline
- Five or more years of professional data science experience in manufacturing and/or supply chain environments
- Hands-on experience working with ERP data and operational processes including order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, and demand-to-deliver
- Proven ability to communicate complex or technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
- Advanced proficiency in Python, SQL and PySpark; experience wrangling large relational datasets
- Deep expertise in machine learning, forecasting and optimization
- Experience deploying enterprise-scale solutions in cloud environments (AWS experience preferred)
- Proficient in at least one data visualization library such as Matplotlib, Seaborn, or Plotly
- Knowledge of Streamlit for building interactive data applications preferred
- Familiarity with MLOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and model lifecycle management
- Demonstrated ability to drive business impact and ROI through analytics
- Strong track record of executive-level communication and influence
- Experience leading cross-functional teams across multiple geographies

results.title: Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
highlights:
Senior Machine Learning Scientist – NLP People
[...]
Senior Data Scientist
[...]
🦸‍♀️ The Role 🦸‍♀️
[...]
Our mission is to help large successful brands like Uber, Amazon, Wise, HelloFresh (and more!) put their customers at the centre of everything they do. Using best-in-class tech in a fast-developing AI space, our Customer Experience Intelligence platform continuously analyses explicit and implicit feedback to enable our clients to identify what they should do next.
[...]
We’re hiring a Senior Data Scientist to join the team and help build and ship the next generation of that stack.
[...]
Unlike many companies, we use our own custom models, specialised for customer feedback, across various parts of the stack: extraction, retrieval, reranking, summarisation, and sentiment analysis. We are also pragmatic and understand that the right solution can be a combination of off-the-shelf LLMs, bespoke fine-tuned models, and sometimes techniques that utilise no LLM at all.
[...]
Train, evaluate, and iterate on ML models and agentic systems for customer feedback, including owning our custom fine-tuning pipelines. Run experiments end-to-end, track results rigorously, and make clear recommendations on what to ship, iterate, or retire.
[...]
Build and maintain LLM-powered features: retrieval pipelines, reranking systems, insight agents, data mining agents, and automated taxonomy generation.
[...]
Design and run robust evaluation frameworks: build test sets, define metrics, evaluate non-deterministic systems, handle class imbalance, and automate checkpoint comparisons.
[...]
Improve and extend semantic search and retrieval, evolving from embedding-based approaches toward more advanced methods.
[...]
Write production-quality code and collaborate closely with Engineering on productionisation, model serving, data pipelines, and monitoring.
[...]
Work with Product and Commercial teams to translate business needs into practical ML solutions, and support client evaluations and accuracy benchmarking.
[...]
Mentor team members,
[...]
🧰 What you’ll need:
[...]
A deep working knowledge of transformer architectures.
[...]
Strong PyTorch skills, with the ability to write custom training loops, modify model architectures, and debug issues at the tensor level. Ideally, experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques such as LoRA
[...]
Extensive experience working with large-scale, messy real-world text data, including classification, extraction, embeddings, re-rankers, clustering, and search.
[...]
Experience in instruction fine-tuning and serving language models, familiarity with frameworks such as vLLM, DeepSpeed, or similar tools
[...]
A solid grounding in classical ML and statistics, and the judgement to choose simpler methods when they’re the right solution.
[...]
Practical experience building with GenAI and agentic patterns.
[...]
Technical curiosity and a keen interest in AI – a love of experimenting to make the most of available technology.
[...]
MSc/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computational Linguistics or a closely related STEM field.

Experience with reinforcement learning techniques, such as with verifiable reward (RLVR)

Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
in machine learning
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning

# Director of Data Analytics & Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions

Job Description SummaryWe are seeking a dynamic, forward-thinking and results-driven Director of Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence to lead our artificial intelligence and machine learning strategy, execution, and team development. Reporting to the CTO, this role will define the direction of AI/ML initiatives, ensuring the development of scalable, high-performance AI solutions that drive innovation and create business value. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background, experience leading AI/ML teams, and the ability to align AI strategies with business objectives. If you are passionate about leveraging AI to transform industries and motivated by the challenge of pushing technological boundaries, we invite you to apply and be part of our journey to redefine what's possible in energy.

results.title: AI Engineer | Zenvok
highlights: AI Engineer responsible for implementing, testing, and supporting generative AI applications. The role involves building workflows, integrations, and orchestration logic to enable large language models to interact with enterprise data and business processes securely and reliably. Responsibilities Implement and support generative AI applications and agentic workflows for enterprise and business platforms, following approved reference architectures and standards Build orchestration logic for multi-step tasks (tool usage, API calls, retrieval workflows, approvals/human-in-the-loop steps) as designed by the AI Solutions Architect & Technical Lead Develop and maintain integrations between AI services and internal systems, structured/unstructured data sources, and approved enterprise or third-party APIs Implement prompt pipelines, structured output handling, evaluations, and automated tests to improve quality, reliability, and auditability Instrument solutions for observability (logging/metrics/tracing) and collaborate on performance and cost optimization Translate solution designs into working code by implementing components, services, and pipelines aligned to security, compliance, and governance guardrails Support user testing and iteration by partnering with product and business stakeholders to refine agent behavior, output quality, and user experience Create and maintain technical documentation (runbooks, integration notes, test plans, and deployment steps) to support reliable delivery and operations Troubleshoot and remediate production issues related to model outputs, grounding/retrieval, orchestration behavior, or API interactions Contribute reusable components and templates (connectors, evaluators, agent scaffolding) and follow established corporate coding standards and review practices Participate in code reviews and technical learning, incorporating feedback from senior engineers to improve engineering quality and delivery speed Skills Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field, or equivalent practical experience 1–3 years of professional software engineering experience (or equivalent internships/co-ops/project work), with some hands-on exposure to AI/ML or LLM-based applications Strong programming skills in Python and experience building backend services and API integrations Experience consuming LLM services via APIs/SDKs and working with at least one common LLM framework or orchestration library Exposure to generative AI application development using common LLM patterns (prompting, retrieval, structured outputs, evaluation, and workflow orchestration) Experience building and deploying applications in AWS (or similar cloud), including familiarity with IAM, networking basics, and managed services Strong understanding of software engineering fundamentals, including testing, version control, CI/CD, debugging, and secure development practices Ability to operate effectively in a regulated environment with strong attention to quality, traceability, and control requirements Familiarity with financial services, asset management, or document-intensive business processes Experience with Amazon Bedrock, AWS SDKs, Lambda, Step Functions, or related AWS-native AI implementation patterns Interest in (or early experience with) agentic systems, multi-step workflows, and tool-calling patterns Familiarity with knowledge bases, vector search, retrieval pipelines, and AI output evaluation methods Experience supporting use cases in investments, compliance, marketing, operations, client servicing, or RFP-related functions Benefits Participation in an incentive program linked to performance (for example, annual discretionary bonus programs, or other annual or non-annual incentive or sales plans) Company Overview Allspring Global Investments is a provider of financial services. results.title: Horseshoe wells expand development options | spiritnow stories
highlights:
In the Eagle Ford, ConocoPhillips is applying new approaches to develop complex units more efficiently.
[...]
The recent deployment of horseshoe wells — extended laterals drilled in a U-shaped configuration — is helping reduce costs and reach resources that are difficult to access with traditional designs. Horseshoe wells are one of several tools the Lower 48 business unit uses to optimize development across its unconventional portfolio.
[...]
- Significant cost savings and capital efficiency: By replacing multiple short, straight wells with fewer, longer horseshoe wells, ConocoPhillips reduced total well count, accelerated development timelines, lowered environmental impact by reducing the number of surface locations, captured comparable resource volumes, and saved tens of millions of dollars in capital expenditures on key projects.
[...]
- Improved resource
[...]
and project economics: Horseshoe wells increase reservoir contact in geologically complex or constrained areas, lowering cost-of-supply by an estimated $1
[...]
–$1
[...]
per barrel of oil equivalent (BOE) and delivering multi-
[...]
-dollar increases in net present value compared with traditional designs.
[...]
- Enabling development of challenging inventory: This approach allows ConocoPhillips to efficiently develop units that would otherwise be uneconomic or stranded because of faults, irregular lease geometry or other constraints, unlocking value in the Eagle Ford and potentially beyond.
[...]
Trevor Hurd,
[...]
ConocoPhillips is now evaluating where horseshoe well designs can be applied beyond the Eagle Ford, with the goal of extending these drilling and completion efficiencies across the broader Lower 48 portfolio.
[...]
By incorporating horseshoe wells as a scalable development option, the company aims to unlock incremental value, drive sustainable capital efficiency and support disciplined, technology‑driven growth in its unconventional resource plays.
[...]
ConocoPhillips sees horseshoe wells as a scalable tool to improve capital efficiency and returns.
[...]
### Q&A / Nick Olds on unlocking Lower 48 results through scale, inventory and tech
